title: romulea tortuosa subsp. aurea
intro: Romulea tortuosa subsp. aurea, also known as the golden Romulea, is a flowering plant species within the Iridaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits associated with Romulea tortuosa subsp. aurea include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can contribute to overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the roots in hot water or using the plant's extracts in various forms, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
